text: Passing score, everywhere. 85% — every module Assessment AND the Comprehensive Exam. “85 or you wait.” One number, no exceptions.
Comprehensive Exam format. 25 questions · 90 minutes · Teams · proctored · cameras on · monthly · 85% to pass. “25 & 90, cameras on” — a webcam with 25/90 taped on it.
Requirement effective date. November 7, 2025 (Priority 1 & 2 applicants). 11/7 — the day the rule ‘switched on.’
The 3 regulation chapters. 105 = general · 46 = children's residential · 115 = human rights. “105 everything, 46 kids, 115 rights.”
Reg 450 / 520 / 620. 450 = training · 520 = risk management · 620 = quality improvement. Count up: 4-Train, 5-Risk, 6-Quality.
Conditional license limit. 6 months per issuance · 12 successive months max combined. 6 + 6 = 12.
Priority pull times. P1 (green) 5–10 business days · P2 (blue) 21 business days · Non-priority = year+. “1 ~10, 2 21.”
Deficiency revision windows. Priority 30 calendar days · Non-priority 90 calendar days. Priority 30, Non-priority 90 (calendar).
8 required documents. service desc · SCC cert · org chart · 90-day financials · budget · staffing plan · position descriptions · policies. “Ate (8) docs for every service.”
Closure vs Denial. Missed deadline = CLOSURE (reapply) · false info / no occupancy / co-location = DENIAL. Deadline = Close · Dishonest = Deny.
Physical environment numbers. Air 65–80°F · hot water 100–120°F · room height 7.5 ft (added after 9/19/2002). Air low, water high: 65–80 / 100–120.
CAP timing. Due 15 business days · one extension +10 business days (before due date) · revised CAP 10 business days. “15 to submit, +10 to extend.”
5 CAP statuses. Complete-No Violations · Pending · Issued · Returned · Approved. The odd one out is ‘Returned.’
“Returned” means. It's WITH the Office of Licensing for review (not back to you). “Returned = their turn.”
4 required points of a CAP response. (1) issue + fix (2) systemic change (3) monitor by title + frequency (4) completion dates. Fix it · Fix the system · Who watches · By when.
Background check: 15 business days. Fingerprints submitted within 15 business days of hire (adult/private side). 15 to fingerprint.
Eligibility letter window. Accessible in CONNECT only 6 months — print & file or repeat the check. Grab it — it disappears at 6 months.
Incident levels reporting. Level I: quarterly QI review · Level II & III: CHRIS within 24 hours. “II & III 24.”
Level III incidents. Death · sexual assault · suicide attempt (admitted, non-emergency) w/ hospital admission. III = the worst three: Death, Sexual assault, Suicide-w/-admission.
Root cause analysis. Within 30 days of discovery (Level II & on-site/in-service Level III). Report in 24h, dig in within 30 days.
2 crisis services NOT licensed. Regional Crisis Call Centers & CITACs. “Call centers & CITACs = unlicensed.”
CRC vs CSU. CRC = up to 23:59, recliners · CSU = residential, 16 beds, 3–5 days. CRC ‘under a day’; CSU ‘16-bed cap.’
OHR does NOT. investigate every complaint · run a 24/7 hotline · give emergency care · remove anyone. OHR oversees; it doesn’t rescue.
Only pre-licensure human rights policy. Complaint resolution policy (12VAC35-115-175). One policy first: complaints (175).
HCBS 3 agencies. CMS (federal $) · DMAS (state Medicaid $ + agreement) · DBHDS (day-to-day + licensing). DMAS = Dollars.
Can never be modified (HCBS). Physical accessibility. You can’t modify the ramp.
Exam registration. Email the Exam Registration form + scanned photo ID (same email) to OLTraining@dbhds.virginia.gov. Form + ID, one email, same address.
One number runs the whole thing…. pass every module. pass the final.
85%.. Same bar everywhere.
The final: 25 questions.. 90 minutes. One sitting.
On Microsoft Teams . Cameras ON the entire time.
“85 or wait.”. 25 & 90, cameras on.
5 regions, 5 colors . they love asking this.
1 NW · 2 North · 3 SW . …4 Central · 5 East
Two rulebooks.. 105 = general · 46 = kids.
Conditional license?. 6 + 6 = 12 months max
“105 everything, 46 kids.”. Sponsored home = couch for 2.
Three regs to tattoo on your brain .
450 = Training.
520 = Risk.
620 = Quality.
Subscribe to the email list . miss a change = rejection.
Count up: 4-Train · 5-Risk · 6-Quality.. Not subscribed = surprised = rejected.
Priority = speed..
P1 green 5–10 business days .
P2 blue 21 business days .
Non-priority a year+ .
Fix deficiencies:. Priority 30 · Non-priority 90 (calendar).
“1 ~10, 2 21.”. Priority 30, Non-priority 90.
Eight docs. Every service.. “Ate (8) documents.”
desc · SCC · org · $$$. …budget · staffing · positions · policies
Money = 90 days of expenses . statement < 90 days old.
Miss a deadline CLOSE.. Lie DENY.
Deadline = Close · Dishonest = Deny.. Two 90s: 90 days cash, statement < 90 days.
CONNECT = the online portal.. live since 2021.
Pick your service carefully….
…once you submit, it’s LOCKED . no take-backs.
Message in the portal.. it’s on the record.
“Submit = sealed.”. Message in-portal = documented.
Two magic ranges..
Hot water: 100–120°F.
After the conditional period?. every visit is unannounced
Air low, water high.. 65–80 / 100–120. After conditional = surprise.
Cited? You owe a CAP..
Due in 15 business days..
One extension: +10 business days.. ask BEFORE it’s due.
Three parts:. what you’ll fix · by when · who’s responsible.
“15 to submit, +10 to extend.”. Fix · By-when · Who.
Plot twist:. ‘Returned’ back to you.
‘Returned’ = it’s WITH Licensing.. they’re reviewing it.
Save Submit.. click SUBMIT CAP or it doesn’t count.
Names? Never. . roles & titles only (HIPAA).
“Returned = their turn.”. Save Submit.
3 flavors of change..
Service · Location · Information.
Info changes: anytime.. the others: plan 30 days ahead.
Onsite inspection only for:. Residential · Inpatient · MAT.
Info = anytime; big = 30 days early.. Inspection triggers = R.I.M.
Fingerprints: 15 business days.. from hire date.
Name shows in CONNECT: 24 hours..
Eligibility letter?. print it — gone in 6 months.
Kids’ facilities:. results BEFORE anyone works.
15 fingerprint · 24 appear · 6-mo grab.. Kids = results first.
Levels I, II, III.. know the jump.
Level I:. not in CHRIS — review quarterly.
Level II & III:. CHRIS within 24 hours
Level III =. Death · Sexual assault · Suicide-w/-admission.
“II & III 24.”. III = the worst three.
Crisis Now, 3 doors:. Call · Respond · Go-to
Two services NOT licensed:. Call Centers & CITACs.
CRC = up to 23:59 . recliners, not beds.
CSU = 16 beds max . over 16 no Medicaid (21–64).
Call centers & CITACs = unlicensed.. 16 beds is the line.
OHR guards rights…. …but it doesn’t rescue.
No 24/7 hotline.. Doesn’t investigate every complaint. Can’t remove anyone.
Abuse/neglect . CHRIS in 24 hours.
One policy before licensure:. complaint resolution (175).
OHR oversees; providers investigate.. ANE = 24h.
Same life as everyone else.. that’s the whole rule.
3 agencies:. CMS (fed $) · DMAS (state $) · DBHDS (day-to-day).
Provider home =. lease · lock · roommate choice · food + visitors anytime.
Never modifiable:. physical accessibility
“DMAS = Dollars.”. Can’t modify the ramp.
Two items, one email..
Registration form + scanned photo ID. OLTraining@dbhds.virginia.gov
Register with the SAME email.. or it’s rejected.
The Join link comes once.. save it. A retake = a new version.
Form + ID, one email, same address.. Retake = remix.

Closure vs Denial. Closure: Missed a deadline, Administrative, You CAN reapply. Versus Denial: False/misleading info, No valid cert of occupancy, Improper co-location. Key: Deadline = close · Dishonest = deny .

Level II vs Level III incident. Level II: Serious injury, ER visit, Missing person, Unplanned admission, Choking w/ physical intervention, Decubitus / bowel obstruction / aspiration pneumonia. Versus Level III: Any death, Sexual assault, Suicide attempt w/ hospital admission, Counts even OFF-premises. Key: Both CHRIS in 24h . III = the worst three: Death, Sexual assault, Suicide-with-admission.

CRC vs CSU. CRC (23-hour): Up to 23:59, Center-based, Recliners, not beds, Observe & assess. Versus CSU (residential): Short-term residential, 16 beds, Typically 3–5 days. Key: Over 16 beds = federal IMD (no Medicaid ages 21–64).

Priority 1 vs Priority 2. Priority 1 (green): Essential, big unmet need, Pulled in 5–10 business days. Versus Priority 2 (blue): Benefits from development, Pulled in 21 business days. Key: “1 ~10, 2 21.” Non-priority = a year+.
The three different 90s. 90 = money: Evidence of 90 days of operating expenses, Bank statement cash vs 90-day deadlines .

Conditional vs Annual/Triennial. Conditional: The FIRST license, 6 mo each · 12 mo max, No adding services/locations, Must serve to prove compliance. Versus Annual / Triennial: Full compliance shown, Annual = 1 year, Triennial = 3 years. Key: Conditional is the starting gate; annual/triennial are earned.

Policy Review vs Licensing Specialist. Policy Review Specialist: Reviews compliance FIRST, Sends deficiency letters. Versus Licensing Specialist: Assigned AFTER revisions accepted, Runs the on-site inspection. Key: Paper first (Policy), people second (Licensing).

Background checks: Private vs Children's. Private provider: Direct care + their supervisors, Fingerprints 15 business days, Can start while pending*. Versus Children's facility: EVERYONE: employees, volunteers, contractors, Results required BEFORE working. Key: Kids = everyone + results first; adults = direct care + supervisors.

105 vs 46 vs 115. 12VAC35-105 / -46: 105 = general regs (most services), 46 = children's residential. Versus 12VAC35-115: Human Rights Regulations, Enforced by the Office of Human Rights. Key: 105 everything · 46 kids · 115 rights.

CMS vs DMAS vs DBHDS. CMS / DMAS: CMS = federal funder & oversight, DMAS = state Medicaid $ + participation agreement. Versus DBHDS: Day-to-day waiver operations, Licenses many providers. Key: DMAS = dollars ; DBHDS = day-to-day + licensing.

CAP status: Returned vs Issued. Returned: You submitted it, It's WITH the Office of Licensing, You can't view it. Versus Issued: Needs your response, Also the status of a re-issued CAP. Key: “Returned = their turn.” Save Submit.
